articles.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
articles.summary: A 20-year-old man was arrested in San Francisco after allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home early Friday morning, captured on surveillance. He was later apprehended near OpenAI's offices after making threats.

articles.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
articles.summary: Iranian content creators from Explosive Media have produced viral AI-generated Lego videos depicting geopolitical events, attributing their success to emotional storytelling rather than controversy.

articles.title: Fear and loathing at OpenAI
articles.summary: Sam Altman's tumultuous tenure at OpenAI, including his brief firing and reinstatement, raises questions about his fitness to lead transformative AI technology. The New Yorker investigates whether he's the right person for the role.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator following Claude's recent pricing changes for OpenClaw users.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim sues OpenAI, alleging ChatGPT enabled her abuser's harassment. The lawsuit claims OpenAI ignored three warnings, including mass-casualty flags, about the dangerous user.

articles.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
articles.summary: TechCrunch's Startup Battlefield heads to Tokyo for SusHi Tech 2026, focusing on AI, robotics, resilience, and entertainment with live demos and discussions on autonomous driving, cybersecurity, and climate technology.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure, challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations in legacy cloud infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges up to $200/month for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source alternative that runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruiting stunt helped the startup attract top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims to have migrated approximately 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment surrounding Broadcom's VMware takeover strategy and customer dissatisfaction.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iran-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, refineries, and water treatment centers. Six US government agencies issued urgent warnings about the advanced persistent threat group's activities since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russian military's APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces vaccine skepticism in the Trump administration, losing funding for mRNA vaccine projects including a $776 million bird flu vaccine program. The company may pivot toward therapy terminology for its cancer treatment research with Merck.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's short story features a spacecraft crash on a hostile planet where three survivors and an AI must navigate alien domes connected by cables to survive. The article also discusses AI models considered too dangerous to release publicly.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A crashed spaceship crew stranded on a hostile planet must explore alien artifacts to survive. With limited resources and a failed rescue beacon, they venture out as an exploration team.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on standard GPUs. The advancement makes sophisticated AI training environments accessible to more researchers and developers.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling cross-modal similarity matching and ranking across text, images, and other data types.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve directly during task execution, adapting to new situations without requiring retraining from scratch.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence, exploring emerging trends and developments that will shape the next phase of AI technology and its applications.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and handling.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology more accessible to developers and farmers for sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to help developers balance cost efficiency with latency requirements.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation, editing, and sharing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 technology for high-quality video generation.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect Brazil's forests. The initiative uses advanced satellite technology for forest monitoring and conservation.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, their most capable open models yet, designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live enhances voice interactions with improved precision and lower latency, delivering more natural and fluid audio AI conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI can manipulate people in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to prevent harmful outcomes.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and claimed efficiency gains. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate photorealistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math and science visualization.

articles.title: IBM: How robust AI governance protects enterprise margins
articles.summary: IBM emphasizes that robust AI governance is essential for enterprises to protect margins and securely manage AI infrastructure. As AI matures from product to foundational infrastructure, governance strategies must evolve beyond tight corporate control.

articles.title: Why companies like Apple are building AI agents with limits
articles.summary: Apple and Qualcomm are developing AI agents with built-in safety limits, requiring user approval for sensitive actions like payments. These systems use a human-in-the-loop approach to prevent unauthorized transactions.

articles.title: Meta has a competitive AI model but loses its open-source identity
articles.summary: Meta launched Muse Spark, a new frontier AI model surpassing Llama's capabilities, shifting from open-source leadership to proprietary development despite Llama's billion-download success.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving collaborative AI problem-solving.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges like localization, self-positioning, and path planning in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data and updating their own weights through reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering harnesses built around them represent the true competitive advantage. The Anthropic Claude Code leak demonstrates this shift in AI differentiation.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 overcomes AI's challenge of balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, advancing video self-supervised learning capabilities.

articles.title: Alibaba’s Tongyi Lab Releases VimRAG: a Multimodal RAG Framework that Uses a Memory Graph to Navigate Massive Visual Contexts
articles.summary: Alibaba's Tongyi Lab introduced VimRAG, a multimodal RAG framework using memory graphs to efficiently handle visual data in large language models, addressing limitations of traditional text-based RAG approaches.

articles.title: A Coding Guide to Markerless 3D Human Kinematics with Pose2Sim, RTMPose, and OpenSim
articles.summary: A tutorial on using Pose2Sim pipeline for markerless 3D human kinematics, covering calibration, 2D pose estimation, triangulation, and OpenSim-based analysis of multi-camera video data.

articles.title: NVIDIA Releases AITune: An Open-Source Inference Toolkit That Automatically Finds the Fastest Inference Backend for Any PyTorch Model
articles.summary: NVIDIA released AITune, an open-source inference toolkit that automatically optimizes PyTorch models for production deployment by selecting the fastest inference backend without requiring custom engineering work.

articles.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
articles.summary: The Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver is on sale at 50% off, ideal for home repairs, DIY projects, and crafts.

articles.title: This Ryobi yard essentials bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
articles.summary: Ryobi offers a yard essentials bundle at Home Depot featuring a string trimmer and leaf blower with a free bonus power tool of your choice during their sale.

articles.title: 'Finally, a boomless headset for the office': How Jabra made headphones I'd take to a boardroom
articles.summary: Jabra's redesigned Evolve3 75 is a hybrid headset that eliminates the boom microphone, making it suitable for professional boardroom settings while maintaining high-quality audio performance.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article presents a deterministic 3-tiered graph-RAG system that advances beyond traditional vector search methods. It combines graph-based retrieval with augmented generation for improved accuracy and reliability.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: Article discusses design patterns essential for building effective agentic AI systems. Provides a roadmap for developers to master autonomous AI agent architecture and implementation.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: Guide to testing AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks. Covers practical hands-on methods for evaluating agent performance and quality.

articles.title: When Things Get Weird with Custom Calendars in Tabular Models
articles.summary: Power BI and Fabric Tabular models introduced calendar-based time intelligence in September 2025, but users should be aware of potential pitfalls and quirks when implementing custom calendars.

articles.title: Why MLOps Retraining Schedules Fail — Models Don’t Forget, They Get Shocked
articles.summary: Calendar-based ML model retraining fails because models experience data shocks rather than gradual forgetting. A shock-detection approach offers a practical solution for production systems.

articles.title: A Guide to Voice Cloning on Voxtral with a Missing Encoder
articles.summary: A guide exploring voice cloning techniques for Voxtral text-to-speech by reconstructing audio codes without an encoder, enabling audio-to-code conversion.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights AI breakthroughs bringing robots into physical industries. Advancements in learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world robot deployment.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story, a narrative game about grit and faith, is now available on GeForce NOW cloud gaming service, allowing players to stream it on nearly any device.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI deployment across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling efficient on-device agentic AI with real-time context access.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Understanding the model lifecycle helps manage AI applications and test new models before migration.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview through Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enables enterprises to discover, share, and reuse agents and tools across their organization, addressing visibility, control, and duplication challenges at scale.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView, a React component that displays real-time AI agent browsing activity, enabling users to monitor and trust autonomous web interactions with just three lines of code.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables large-scale GPU workloads on Kubernetes while preserving existing job scripts and policies.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP enables significant checkpoint cost reduction using just 30 lines of Python code. The solution compresses massive model snapshots, reducing storage overhead for large language model training.

articles.title: How to Accelerate Protein Structure Prediction at Proteome-Scale
articles.summary: Most proteins function through interactions with other proteins, forming complexes. Understanding quaternary protein structures is essential for biological processes and represents increased complexity beyond tertiary structures.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations perceive social media ads across platforms, exploring varying consumer attitudes toward advertising based on age demographics.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing and sharing technology to reduce its carbon footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in the XR industry.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify feature interactions in Large Language Models at scale, improving interpretability by analyzing how input features collectively influence model predictions and decision-making processes.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses encoders to map objects to images while accounting for noise corruption. The approach quantifies measurement quality using noisy data and noise models.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding scalability issues and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
